lks2.objective: Play 1v1 matches in your tunnel and learn to show respect — to opponents and to yourself — when you win and when you lose.
successCriteria: Moves up or down the tunnels without protest

uks2.objective: Compete in fast 1v1 duals — score, defend, restart yourself — and demonstrate sportsmanship after every result.

space.description: Playing area split into multiple small 1v1 'tunnels' with cones. Each tunnel has a coned goal at either end. Pair the children up so each tunnel has one 1v1 game going at once.

value.name: Respect
value.text: Respect is about how we behave when we win and lose. Stay humble, show good sportsmanship and acknowledge your opponent after every game — especially when you win. Respect should show at all times, but it shows most after the result.

steps: Set up multiple narrow tunnels with cone goals at each end.
Pair children — one game per tunnel.
Children manage their own game — restarts, decisions, kick-offs — no coach intervention.
After each game: handshake or similar gesture before moving on.
Winner moves UP a tunnel (to a harder pairing). Loser moves DOWN.
progressions: Change scoring: stop the ball under control on the goal line to score (cleaner technique).
Add a 'playmaker' on each pitch to create a 2v1 overload for whoever has possession.
questions: What did your opponent do well?
How did you behave after winning? After losing?
What's one thing you'd try differently next game?

step.space: Reduce tunnel size to increase pressure and quick decision-making; increase to allow more space to attack
step.task: Knock a football off a cone to score (instead of shooting into a goal)
step.equipment: Adjust the size of the goals to increase or decrease difficulty
step.people: Rotate opponents regularly and adjust pairings to create appropriate challenges

base.showmanship: Use this session to highlight behaviour in winning moments. Watch closely for how children react after scoring — praise children who show respect, acknowledge their opponent and reset quickly. Parents should hear that the focus is on HOW they behave, not just whether they win.
